reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
It's a Thursday in early June. Nothing special about it. The kind of day that used to disappear into the blur — wake up rough, push through work, start drinking by six, lose the evening, repeat. Thursdays were placeholders. Bridges to the weekend, which was the only thing that mattered, and even the weekend wasn't really for living. It was for getting obliterated.

The strange thing about getting sober is how much weight a regular Thursday starts to carry. You make coffee. You actually taste it. You go outside and the light is different in June than it was in May, and you notice. You text a friend back. You go for a run, or you don't, but either way the choice is yours. None of this sounds like much. None of it would have impressed the 22-year-old version of me who thought a real life had to be loud to count.

But Durant's right — we are what we repeatedly do. The life I have now isn't the result of one big decision. It's the accumulation of a thousand ordinary Thursdays where I showed up sober and let the day be what it was. Skydiving, ski seasons, hunting trips, the friendships — those are the highlights. The foundation is Thursday. The foundation is now.
reflection.meditation: What would it mean to let today be enough, exactly as it is?

affirmations: I am building a life one ordinary day at a time, and today is one of those days.
Today I get to choose how I respond, not just react.
I am someone I can live with, and that's worth more than any escape ever was.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a quiet Thursday morning that I can actually remember. Grateful for the people who stayed when I was hard to love, and the ones I've met on this side of the drink. Grateful that the small things — coffee, sunlight, a clear head — aren't small at all when you've spent years numb to them.
gratitude.closing: One more day. That's the whole job.
